name: San Marino
avoid: Midday summer weekends when day-trippers from the Riviera crowd the historic center and tower path.
Foggy winter days when Mount Titano views disappear and the ridge walk feels less rewarding.
Fridays in Borgo Maggiore only if you dislike market crowds around Piazza Grande and the lower cable-car station.
stopoverNote: San Marino is easy as a day trip from Rimini, but 2 nights lets you see the towers, museums and lower towns without rushing or relying only on midday buses.

cities.name: San Marino City
cities.notes: UNESCO-listed hilltop capital for the Three Towers path, Palazzo Pubblico, basilica viewpoints and the republic's densest cluster of museums.

cities.name: Borgo Maggiore
cities.notes: Lower town at the foot of Mount Titano with the cable car, Piazza Grande market area and some of the easiest-value stays in the republic.

cities.name: Serravalle
cities.notes: San Marino's biggest castello, practical for shopping, football at Stadio Olimpico and a last-night stay closer to the Rimini side of the border.

itinerary.sim: Use an Italy/EU eSIM or roaming pack; San Marino is tiny and most visitors enter from Rimini, so an Italy-valid plan plus offline maps is usually enough.

San Marino City.name: San Marino City
San Marino City.note: Give the capital a full day for the core monuments and ridge walk; add a second only if you want a slower museum pace, sunset viewpoints and a nicer dinner.

days.theme: Three Towers, state history and the classic Mount Titano skyline

activities.name: Bonelli Bus shuttle from Rimini railway station to San Marino
activities.tip: The direct coach is the simplest car-free arrival; book the first morning departure in high season to beat tower crowds.

activities.name: San Marino Multi-Museum Pass for the Three Towers circuit, State Museum and Palazzo Pubblico when open
activities.tip: The current adult pass is about €11 and is better value than paying only for the two main towers if you also want the museum and public palace.

activities.name: Guaita and Cesta towers ridge walk, with Montale Tower exterior viewpoints on the trail
activities.tip: The same museums pass covers Guaita and Cesta; Montale is normally viewed from outside along the ridge path.

activities.name: Palazzo Pubblico and Piazza della Liberta
activities.tip: Palazzo Pubblico entry is included in the museums pass when government functions do not close parts of the building.

activities.name: State Museum of San Marino
activities.tip: Use the same pass here; the museum is compact enough to pair with the towers in one day if you start early.

activities.name: Basilica di San Marino, Passo delle Streghe and old-town lanes

Borgo Maggiore.name: Borgo Maggiore
Borgo Maggiore.note: Borgo Maggiore works best as a slower half- to full-day extension: ride the cable car, browse the lower town and stay here if you want easier parking or better-value hotels.

days.theme: Cable car panoramas, Friday market energy and lower-town local life

activities.name: Funivia di San Marino cable car between Borgo Maggiore and San Marino City (return)
activities.tip: The current adult round-trip fare is about €7; ride it early or near sunset for the best valley views.

activities.name: Piazza Grande and the Borgo Maggiore market area
activities.tip: Friday is the main market day, so visit then if you want the liveliest local atmosphere.

activities.name: Church of Sant'Antimo e San Marino and old lower-town lanes
Local bus ride within San Marino
activities.tip: A standard local one-way bus ticket is about €2 and is useful if you do not want to tackle the steep roads on foot.

activities.name: Walk back through the lower viewpoints or return by cable car

Serravalle.name: Serravalle
Serravalle.note: Use Serravalle for a practical last day before heading back to Rimini: shopping, sports venues and a lower-altitude overnight near the border road.

days.theme: Modern San Marino: shopping district, stadium stop and easy exit day

activities.name: Local bus from San Marino City or Borgo Maggiore to Serravalle
activities.tip: Serravalle is only a short hop away by local bus, and buses save you from arranging taxis in a country where distances are tiny but uphill roads are constant.

activities.name: Stadio Olimpico di Serravalle exterior and sports district walk
Atlante Shopping Center or Azzurro Shopping Center browse
San Marino Outlet Experience detour by bus or taxi if you want brand shopping
activities.tip: Window-shopping is free; add only your transport cost if you do not have a car.

activities.name: Bonelli Bus return from the Rimini-side corridor

connections.method: Direct Bonelli Bus shuttle from Rimini railway station up to San Marino's historic center
Ride the Funivia cable car or walk down the steep road and stairways
connections.cost: ₹630 pp return by cable car or ₹0 on foot

connections.method: Local bus across the republic or a short taxi ride if you are carrying luggage
connections.cost: ₹180 pp by bus or about ₹1,350–₹1,800 by taxi

connections.method: Return by Bonelli Bus toward Rimini or connect onward by road to the Adriatic coast

extras: San Marino is tiny enough that confident walkers can cover a lot on foot, but the climbs between levels are steeper than the map suggests.
If you only have one day, focus on San Marino City and use the cable car once for Borgo Maggiore rather than trying to sleep in multiple towns.
The museums pass is the key value buy because it bundles the towers with the State Museum and Palazzo Pubblico instead of making you choose just one sight.
Stay in Borgo Maggiore or Serravalle if you want easier bus logistics and cheaper hotels than the hilltop historic center.
